item.title: [General] Should you use AI Max?
item.description:
My honest take on AI Max for Search campaigns (recorded April 2026):
I think it has potential. But right now, I’m not prepared to put my name behind it. And here’s the thing most people aren’t saying out loud: the vast majority of businesses don’t need AI Max yet.
You can scale profitably with thi

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Data Studio is Back!?!
item.description:
Google has announced that Looker Studio is now Data Studio. The change includes a new logo, updated interface and new conversational analysis feature. Learn more about the new Google Data Studio in this video.
Join our courses: https://www.lovesdata.com/courses/ Subscribe to our YouTube channel: ht

item.description: AI compresses the consumer journey. Discover how Bing Search shapes intent, validates options, and drives action at key decision moments.

In this video I take you through the results and the process we used.

item.title: [Meta Ads] Why Meta Keeps Pushing Value Rules
item.description: Meta has spent years removing advertiser control over targeting and placements, so actively encouraging value rules seemed contradictory. But it makes sense when you see where things are headed. Jon explains how a new test version lets you bid based on custom audience labels for lead quality and cus

item.title: [General] Top AI Transcription Tools for Meetings in 2026: 11 Best Options Compared for Accuracy, Notes, and Security
item.description: Meetings create decisions, commitments, risks, and next steps. The problem is that most of that value disappears quickly when notes are incomplete, action items are not captured clearly, or the only record of what happened lives in one person’s memory. That is why AI transcription tools for meeti

item.title: [General] How to Use Zapier: A Step-by-Step Guide to Automating Workflows With AI, Email, CRM, and Jasper
item.description: If you want the short version, here it is: Zapier is a no-code automation platform that connects the tools your team already uses so work moves automatically from one app to another. Instead of copying information from a form into a spreadsheet, sending a Slack alert, creating a CRM contact, and

item.title: [General] Top AI Image Generation Tools in 2026: Best AI Image Generators Compared for Quality, Text Accuracy, Editing, and Commercial Use

The real question is which tool fits the job, the workflow, the brand standard, and the legal

item.title: [Google Ads] How Voice Search Is Changing Google Ads Search Term Reports in 2026: 12 Practical Fixes to Cut Waste and Improve Intent Matching
item.description: If you manage Google Ads closely, you can see the difference immediately in the search term report. The old pattern was compact and predictable. People typed short, compressed phrases like “emergency plumber,” “crm software pricing,” or “best running shoes.” Today, more queries arrive in the form

item.title: [LinkedIn Ads] LinkedIn Advertising: 21 Advanced Tactics to Improve Lead Quality, Lower CPL, and Scale B2B Pipeline
item.description: LinkedIn advertising rewards precision more than volume. One team launches broad campaigns, targets a few job titles, adds a form, and calls the channel expensive. Anot

item.title: [General] Ubersuggest Review and Guide (Updated April 2026): Features, Pricing, Accuracy, Pros, Cons, and Best Uses

item.title: [General] Connected TV Advertising in 2026: How CTV Works, What It Costs, and How to Measure Results
item.description: Connected TV advertising has moved from an experimental media line to a standard part of modern video planning. For many brands, that shift did not happen because CTV was new. It happened because audience behavior changed first. People now watch a large share of TV through streaming environments

item.title: [General] Dominate Local SEO: The Ultimate Pest Control Keyword Strategy #shorts

item.title: [General] デザイナーが広告運用やってみて感じたこと
item.description:
アナグラムでデザイナーをしている私が、広告運用の兼務を始めました。運用経験ゼロからのスタートです。 もちろん日々できることが増えている最中ですが、「兼務」によって得られたものは想像以上でした。この記事…

item.title: [General] Consolidate Ads with the New Creative Workflow

item.description: Client retention in white label SEO is rarely lost in one dramatic moment. Most accounts do not disappear because of a single bad month, one missed keyword target, or one awkward meeting. They leave because several small problems go unaddressed at the same time: expectations start to drift, commu

item.description: Gatorade’s new global football campaign does two things at once. item.title: [General] White Label SEO for Franchises: A Practical Guide to Location Page Architecture, Brand Consistency, and Scalable Citation Management
item.description: Franchise SEO looks straightforward from the outside. A brand has one name, one broad service set, and multiple locations. item.title: [General] How to Pitch White Label SEO to Existing Web Design and PPC Clients: Scripts, Packages, and an Agency Upsell Playbook

item.title: [General] Snapchat Place Loyalty on Snap Map: How Top Visitor Badges Work, What They Mean, and Why This Update Matters

item.description: This April, we're introducing several new API features and improvements to help developers and partners streamline branded content workflows, better measure content performance, access collaborative media, and manage engagement on Instagram. These updates are available through the Instagram API with

item.title: [Google Ads] Google広告のDSAがAI Maxへ移行へ。広告運用者が押さえるべき変化
item.description:
2026年4月15日、GoogleはDSA（ダイナミック検索広告）をAI Maxへ段階的に移行すると発表しました。 参考：Google’s Dynamic Search Ads are upgradi…

The post Google広告のDSAがAI Maxへ移行へ。広告運用者が押さえるべき変化 first appeared on アナグラム株式会社.
item.category: アナグラム ブログ

item.title: [General] Heinz’s ‘Mr. 57’ NFL Draft Campaign – What the 57th Pick, Pittsburgh, and Kraft Heinz’s New NFL Partnership Mean for Brand Marketing
item.description: When Heinz launched its first NFL Draft activation as part of Kraft Heinz’s new league partnership, it did not try to force a complicated message into a fast-moving sports moment. Instead, it did something much harder and much smarter: it found a piece of cultural real estate that already belonge

item.title: [General] Pepsi Football Nation – How David Beckham and Pepsi Built a Fan Platform Around Football Culture, Rivalries, and Everyday Rituals
item.description: Pepsi has spent decades attaching itself to football’s biggest moments, biggest players, and biggest audiences. What makes Pepsi Football Nation different is that it shifts the spotlight away from the standard brand formula of “star players + tournament season + ad film” and toward something more

item.title: [General] YouTube AI Likeness Detection Tool – How It Works, Who Can Use It, and What the 2026 Expansion Means
item.description: Artificial intelligence has changed the speed, cost, and realism of synthetic media. A few years ago, producing a convincing fake video of a public figure took specialized skills, significant time, and access to expensive tools. Today, a capable user can generate a persuasive imitation of a face,

item.title: [General] Snap CFO Steps Down – What Derek Andersen’s Exit and Doug Hott’s Appointment Mean for Snapchat’s Profitability, Ad Business, and AR Plans
item.description: When a chief financial officer leaves a major platform company, the headline is rarely just about one executive. It is usually about timing, priorities, and what the business needs next. That is especially true at Snap, where the finance leadership change arrives at a moment when the company is t

item.title: [General] LinkedIn Crosscheck – How Premium Users Can Compare AI Model Outputs in One Place
item.description: LinkedIn has introduced a new product called Crosscheck, and its purpose is straightforward: help professionals compare AI responses from different model providers inside a single workflow. Instead of asking users to open several tools, manage several subscriptions, and remember which output came

item.title: [General] The Complete White Label Local SEO Stack for Agencies – 12 Services to Resell Beyond Google Business Profile Optimization
item.description: Most agencies start local SEO with the most visible asset in the campaign: the Google Business Profile. That makes sense. It is public, clients can see changes quickly, and it often influences calls, directions, and map visibility faster than a website rebuild or a link campaign. But a Google Bus

item.title: [General] White Label Video Production for Agencies – How to Deliver Commercial-Quality Video at Scale Without Hiring a Videographer
item.description: Video has moved from a nice-to-have asset to a standard client expectation. Brands want launch videos, paid social ads, product explainers, founder interviews, customer testimonials, event recaps, onboarding content, recruiting videos, YouTube assets, short-form social clips, and landing-page vid

item.title: [General] How Agencies Resell Conversion Rate Optimization – A/B Testing, Heatmaps, Landing Page Audits, Pricing, and Delivery
item.description: Every agency eventually runs into the same client question: if traffic is already coming in, why are more of those visitors not turning into leads, demos, purchases, or qualified calls? That question is where conversion rate optimization becomes commercially important. SEO can grow visibility. Pa

item.title: [General] White Label Marketing Automation for Agencies: The CRM Workflows and Lead Nurturing Systems Behind $5K/Month Retainers
item.description: Most agencies do not have a lead problem. They have a follow-up problem, a process problem, or a delivery model problem. They generate traffic, build landing pages, run paid campaigns, and produce content. But once a lead fills out a form, books a call, downloads a guide, abandons a cart, or goes

item.title: [General] White Label Local SEO – Comprehensive Guide to Services, Pricing, Deliverables, and Agency Growth
item.description: Local SEO is one of the most consistently valuable services a marketing agency can sell. It is tied closely to commercial intent, it can be measured through calls, leads, form fills, directions, bookings, and store visits, and it matters for businesses that depend on customers in specific cities,
